body:
and also correct some `Float64` that slipped in the `SplitExplicitFreeSurface`. This is some preparatory work to allow more flexible user type and eventually fully support Metal.

Note that I have tested `Metal` on an additional branch that implements these changes + some (quite easy actually) support for Metal architectures https://github.com/CliMA/Oceananigans.jl/tree/ss/fix-metal (as well as some other tangential stuff I was trying which is not relevant) and this [example](https://github.com/CliMA/Oceananigans.jl/blob/ss/fix-metal/validation/mesoscale_turbulence/baroclinic_adjustment.jl) seem to run quite nicely with WENO, so after this PR is merged, the route for Metal should be
- enable easy Float32 support
- implement a `MetalGPU` (alias for a possible `GPU{<:Metal.MetalBackend}`) architecture
title: Remove all hardcoded Float64 from the `Advection` module

body:
to reproduce the issue on main:
```julia
julia> grid = RectilinearGrid(size = (1, 1), x = (0, 1), z = (0, 1), topology = (Periodic, Flat, Bounded))
1×1×1 RectilinearGrid{Float64, Periodic, Flat, Bounded} on CPU with 1×0×1 halo
├── Periodic x ∈ [0.0, 1.0) regularly spaced with Δx=1.0
├── Flat y
└── Bounded  z ∈ [0.0, 1.0] regularly spaced with Δz=1.0

julia> Oceananigans.Operators.ℑyᵃᶠᵃ(1, 1, 1, grid, 1)
ERROR: MethodError: ℑyᵃᶠᵃ(::Int64, ::Int64, ::Int64, ::RectilinearGrid{Float64, Periodic, Flat, Bounded, Oceananigans.Grids.StaticVerticalCoordinate{OffsetArrays.OffsetVector{Float64, StepRangeLen{Float64, Base.TwicePrecision{Float64}, Base.TwicePrecision{Float64}, Int64}}, Float64}, Float64, Float64, OffsetArrays.OffsetVector{Float64, StepRangeLen{Float64, Base.TwicePrecision{Float64}, Base.TwicePrecision{Float64}, Int64}}, Nothing, CPU}, ::Int64) is ambiguous.

Candidates:
  ℑyᵃᶠᵃ(i, j, k, grid::Oceananigans.Grids.YFlatGrid, c)
    @ Oceananigans.Operators ~/.julia/packages/Oceananigans/HTdbl/src/Operators/interpolation_operators.jl:90
  ℑyᵃᶠᵃ(i, j, k, grid::Oceananigans.Grids.AbstractGrid, f::Number, args...)
    @ Oceananigans.Operators ~/.julia/packages/Oceananigans/HTdbl/src/Operators/interpolation_operators.jl:35

Possible fix, define
  ℑyᵃᶠᵃ(::Any, ::Any, ::Any, ::Oceananigans.Grids.YFlatGrid, ::Number)

Stacktrace:
 [1] top-level scope
   @ REPL[4]:1
```
title: Disambiguate interpolation of `::Number` on grids with `Flat` dimensions
